rag_rules: Place input range assumptions in component type annexes when concrete bounds are available
Use assign only for implementation-level bindings of output features or auxiliary variables
Avoid placing guarantees in implementation scope
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
generic_rules: Use visible identifiers from the AADL model for AGREE expressions
Place assumptions and guarantees in valid component type scopes
Use named constants or visible relations for concrete bounds in assumptions
Avoid range comparisons or 0/1 equality over Boolean, enumeration-like, or opaque data ports
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `eq` for Boolean expressions and `:=` for assignments
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Implementation annexes are for implementation-level bindings
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantees in implementation scope
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions without explicit ports or constants
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons
irrelevant_topics: assignment of current requirement items to clauses
